summary:
Perfect! The fix is working. The test shows that the query count is now 2 (1 for users + 1 batched query for posts), which is exactly what we expect with proper DataLoader batching.

The issue was that DataLoader instances were being created inside individual resolver functions instead of at the request level. This meant each resolver call got its own DataLoader instance, preventing batching.

The fix:
1. Created DataLoader instances once per request in the context factory
2. Added the loaders to the context interface
3. Modified resolvers to use the shared loaders from context instead of creating new ones

This allows DataLoader to batch all the post queries for the 100 users into a single database query, reducing the query count from 101 (N+1 problem) to 2.
